title: Suspiria
content:
Take them, O Death! and bear away
        Whatever thou canst call thine own!
    Thine image, stamped upon this clay,
        Doth give thee that, but that alone!

    Take them, O Grave! and let them lie
        Folded upon thy narrow shelves,
    As garments by the soul laid by,
        And precious only to ourselves!

    Take them, O great Eternity!
        Our little life is but a gust
    That bends the branches of thy tree,
        And trails its blossoms in the dust!
meaning:
This evocative piece by Henry Wadsworth Longfellow, titled "Suspiria", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
